title: Add POST /api/auth/login route handler using AuthService
description: Create a Next.js App Router POST endpoint at src/app/api/auth/login/route.ts that accepts { email, password } JSON body, delegates to AuthService.login(), and returns { accessToken, refreshToken, user } on success or appropriate HTTP error codes (400 for missing fields, 401 for invalid credentials, 403 for inactive accounts). The route handler should reuse the getAuthService() singleton exported from src/auth/withAuth.ts and follow the existing route handler patterns used in src/app/api/. Tests should mock AuthService.login() for unit testing and cover both success and failure cases.
existing_patterns: src/app/api/health/route.ts — pattern: NextRequest import, export const GET/POST handler, Response.json() for responses with explicit status codes and Content-Type headers
src/app/api/csrf-token/route.ts — pattern: singleton service import (getCsrfTokenService), route handler extracts request data and delegates to service

src/auth/withAuth.ts — pattern: getAuthService() singleton exported at line 21, AuthService.login() signature: (email, password, ipAddress, userAgent) returning Promise<AuthResult> with { accessToken, refreshToken, user }
src/auth/auth-service.ts — pattern: service throws Error with .status property for HTTP errors (400, 401, 403)
src/app/api/health/route.test.ts — pattern: vitest unit test mocking service methods with vi.mock, testing request/response shape
